purpose: Standalone IIFE injected into the Live Preview webview via webContents.executeJavaScript(). Enables inline double-click editing of HTML elements bearing data-synapse-label attributes — the in-page side of the !instrument workflow. Scans the DOM for labeled elements, attaches a hover style, and on dblclick converts the element to contentEditable. On commit (Enter or blur) sends a postMessage of shape { type:'synapse-edit', label, newText, oldText, routePath } back to the host. Uses MutationObserver for SPA navigation/React-rerender detection so newly mounted labeled elements pick up styling automatically.
gotchas: Idempotent guard via window.__synapseOverlayInjected — re-injection (e.g. on each navigation) is safe but does NOT update existing handlers. If you change the script you MUST clear the flag or reload.
All CSS classes are 'synapse-' prefixed and CSS custom properties scoped under '--synapse-*' to avoid host-page collisions — DO NOT rename without auditing the host preview/edit flow.
Communication is one-way via window.postMessage — host listens via webContents.on('ipc-message') / window event handler. There is NO synapse-overlay-ready signal back.
MutationObserver is wired to document.body || document.documentElement with subtree:true — high-churn pages cause many debouncedScan invocations (debounce: 100ms).
data-synapse-scanned attribute marks elements already-processed — querySelectorAll filter '[data-synapse-label]:not([data-synapse-scanned])' avoids double-styling.
On Enter (without Shift) the edit commits — Shift+Enter inserts a newline (browser default). Escape cancels and restores original text.
Blur-to-commit uses setTimeout(0) so a focus jump to another labeled element triggers startEdit -> commitEdit ordering correctly (otherwise blur would commit AFTER the new edit started).
findLabeledAncestor walks up to (but not including) document.body — clicking inside a deeply-nested labeled element's child resolves to the labeled ancestor.
newText is taken from element.textContent — HTML formatting inside labeled elements is LOST on edit.
All event listeners use capture phase (true) — overrides host page click handlers reliably.
Activated under window.location.pathname which is sent as routePath in postMessage payload — the host uses this to map the label back to the correct source route.
patterns: IIFE with strict mode and idempotent guard — required for any executeJavaScript-injected script.
CSS custom properties for theme variables inside injected styles — avoids hardcoded colors scattered across selectors.
MutationObserver + debounced scan — the canonical SPA-aware DOM-watching pattern.
postMessage with structured type field — host filters by message.data.type === 'synapse-edit'.
conventions: Var (not let/const) — file uses ES5-compatible syntax for maximum webview compatibility.
All function names use camelCase synapseSomething for any global-leak; locals are short.
